title: Ironworker hand abrasion – first aid only
description: Ironworker sustained minor abrasion to palm of right hand while handling shear studs at Span 2 deck forming area. No PPE violation – gloves worn. First aid applied on site. Worker returned to full duty same day. Incident reviewed at next toolbox talk.
location: West Seattle Bridge – Deck Forming Area Span 2

title: Near miss – unsecured crane load during wind gust
description: During a precast girder pick at Span 3 a gust of 28 mph caused the load to rotate and come within 4 feet of the temporary falsework. Load was lowered safely. Wind monitoring system was functioning and at threshold. Crane operations suspended. Load path re-evaluated before resuming. No injuries.
location: West Seattle Bridge – Marine Crane Barge West Channel

title: Diver nitrogen narcosis – minor
description: During underwater pier inspection at Pier W3 one diver showed signs of mild narcosis at 62 feet. Dive supervisor immediately directed ascent. Diver was safely recovered and assessed by on-site medic. No decompression illness. Dive procedures reviewed and maximum depth for this dive changed to 55 feet for remaining inspection work.
location: West Seattle Bridge – Pier W3 Underwater Inspection

title: Vehicle backing incident – property damage only
description: Concrete truck backing to pump at Ballard Bridge Pier 4 struck temporary jersey barrier while following spotter. No injuries. Two jersey barriers displaced. Spotter communication protocol reviewed and hand signal procedures retrained for all concrete truck drivers.
